content.problemStatement:
Danya has a sequence AAA of non-negative integers. Initially, AAA has length NNN.

Let's define a function f(x,A)f(x, A)f(x,A), where xxx is a positive integer ‚Č§‚ą£A‚ą£\le |A|‚Č§‚ą£A‚ą£, as the maximum of bitwise ANDs of all subsequences of AAA with length equal to xxx.

Kostya gives Danya MMM requests. There are three types of requests:


1‚ÄÖ‚Ääv1\;v1v: append an integer vvv to AAA
2‚ÄÖ‚Ääv2\;v2v: delete an element equal to vvv from AAA; it is guaranteed that AAA contains vvv at least once, but if there are more elements equal to vvv, you should only delete one of them
3‚ÄÖ‚Ääx3\;x3x: calculate f(x,A)f(x, A)f(x,A)
Also, Kostya knows that at any time, all elements of AAA are smaller than 2k2^k2k for a fixed kkk.

Help Danya process all of Kostya's requests.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains three space-separated integers NNN, MMM and kkk.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \dots, a_Na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äč denoting the initial sequence AAA.
Each of the following MMM lines contains two space-separated integers describing a request in the format 1‚ÄÖ‚Ääv1\;v1v, 2‚ÄÖ‚Ääv2\;v2v or 3‚ÄÖ‚Ääx3\;x3x.
content.outputFormat: For each request of type 3, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the value of f(x,A)f(x, A)f(x,A).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Subsequences giving optimal answers for the requests of type 3 are, in order: (6,7)(6, 7)(6,7); (6,7,5)(6, 7, 5)(6,7,5); (6,7,7)(6, 7, 7)(6,7,7); (7,5)(7, 5)(7,5).
